2603.29735.title: Spontaneous Functional Differentiation in Large Language Models: A Brain-Like Intelligence Economy
2603.29735.paper_summary: The evolution of intelligence in artificial systems provides a unique opportunity to identify universal computational principles. Here we show that large language models spontaneously develop synergistic cores where information integration exceeds individual parts remarkably similar to the human brain. Using Integrated Information Decomposition across multiple architectures we find that middle layers exhibit synergistic processing while early and late layers rely on redundancy. This organization is dynamic and emerges as a physical phase transition as task difficulty increases. Crucially ablating synergistic components causes catastrophic performance loss confirming their role as the physical entity of abstract reasoning and bridging artificial and biological intelligence.
2603.29735.paper_summary_zh: 人工系統中智慧的演變提供了一個獨特的機會來識別普遍的計算原則。<lb/>在這裡，我們展示大型語言模型自發地發展出協同核心，<lb/>其資訊整合超過個別部分，與人類大腦驚人地相似。<lb/>通過在多個架構中使用整合資訊分解，我們發現中間層展現出協同處理，而早期和晚期層則依賴於冗餘。<lb/>這種組織是動態的，並隨著任務難度的增加而作為物理相變而出現。<lb/>關鍵是，切除協同組件會導致災難性的性能損失，<lb/>確認了它們作為抽象推理的物理實體以及橋接人工和生物智慧的角色。
2603.29735.author: Junjie Zhang et.al.
2603.29735.authors: Junjie Zhang, Zhen Shen, Gang Xiong, Xisong Dong
